sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 메리어트는 자산 경량화 모델을 통해 67억 4,400만 달러의 매출을 기록했으며, 이자 비용 증가에도 불구하고 견고한 영업 성과를 유지했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 전 세계 30개 이상의 브랜드로 호텔 및 레지던스를 운영하는 자산 경량화(Asset-light) 모델을 통해 안정적인 수수료 수익 구조를 유지하고 있습니다.
RevPAR(객실당 매출), 점유율, ADR(평균 객실 가격)을 핵심 성과 지표로 활용하여 호텔 운영 효율성과 가격 결정력을 관리하고 있습니다.

riskChanges.text: 미래 사업 성과는 개발 파이프라인, 객실 성장률, 유동성 요구 사항 충족 여부 등 경영진의 가정과 예측에 의존하고 있어 불확실성이 존재합니다.

businessChanges.text: 감가상각 및 기타 비용은 2025년 2분기 53백만 달러로 전년 동기 47백만 달러 대비 13% 증가하며 운영 비용 부담이 일부 확대되었습니다.
이자 비용 증가에도 불구하고 이자 수익이 12백만 달러로 전년 동기 9백만 달러 대비 33% 증가하며 금융 수익 측면에서 일부 상쇄 효과를 보였습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025년 6월 30일 기준 매출 67억 4,400만 달러, 영업이익 12억 3,600만 달러, 순이익 7억 6,300만 달러를 달성했습니다.
총자산은 273억 4,200만 달러이며, 현금 및 현금성 자산은 6억 7,100만 달러를 보유하여 안정적인 재무 상태를 유지하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 부채 증가에 따른 이자 비용 상승이 향후 순이익률에 미칠 영향과 자산 경량화 모델 하에서의 지속적인 비용 통제 능력을 모니터링할 필요가 있습니다.

Quarter ended 2025-06-30 net income = 7억 6,300만 달러 (USD 0.763B; 763 million), XBRL range 2025-04-01..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 total assets = 273억 4,200만 달러 (USD 27.342B; 27,342 million), XBRL range instant..2025-06-30.
Quarter ended 2025-06-30 cash and cash equivalents = 6억 7,100만 달러 (USD 0.671B; 671 million), XBRL range instant..2025-06-30.

Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations Cautionary Statement All statements in this report are made as of the date this Form 10-Q is filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (the “SEC”). We undertake no obligation to publicly update or revise these statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise. We make forward-looking statements in Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations and elsewhere in this report based on the beliefs and assumptions of our management and on information available to us through the date this Form 10-Q is filed with the SEC. Forward-looking statements include information related to our development pipeline; our expectations regarding rooms growth; our expectations regarding our ability to meet our liquidity requirements; our capital e

Overview We are a worldwide operator, franchisor, and licensor of hotel, residential, timeshare, and other lodging properties under more than 30 brand names. We discuss our operations in the following reportable business segments: (1) U.S. & Canada, (2) Europe, Middle East & Africa (“EMEA”), (3) Greater China, and (4) Asia Pacific excluding China (“APEC”). Our Caribbean & Latin America (“CALA”) operating segment does not meet the applicable accounting criteria for separate disclosure as a reportable business segment, and as such, we include its results in “Unallocated corporate and other.” Under our asset-light business model, we typically manage or franchise hotels and other lodging offerings, rather than own them. Terms of our management agreements vary, but we earn a management fee that is typically composed of a base management fee, which is a percentage of the revenues of the hotel,

Revenue per Available Room (“RevPAR”), which we calculate by dividing property level room revenue by total rooms available for the period, is a meaningful indicator of our performance because it measures the period-over-period change in room revenues. RevPAR may not be comparable to similarly titled measures, such as revenues, and should not be viewed as necessarily correlating with our fee revenue. We also believe occupancy and average daily rate (“ADR”), which are components of calculating RevPAR, are meaningful indicators of our performance. Occupancy, which we calculate by dividing total rooms sold by total rooms available for the period, measures the utilization of a property’s available capacity. ADR, which we calculate by dividing property level room revenue by total rooms sold, measures average room price and is useful in assessing pricing levels. Unless otherwise stated, RevPAR,
